text: Self-attention has proven to be successful in a variety of computer vision tasks after it was originally proposed for natural language processing applications. Although a self-attention based approach for images provides valuable insights, it has some drawbacks. These include losing spatial information after transforming an image to a 1D sequence, high computational cost due to quadratic operations (especially when dealing with high resolutions) and concentrating on spatial adaptation while leaving channel-wise interaction ignored. This paper introduces Large Kernel Attention (LKA), a linear attention module for exploiting long range relationships and self-adaptation while addressing all of the drawbacks mentioned above. We use LKA for designing the Visual Attention Network (VAN) with excellent performances on a variety of benchmarks. VAN consists of an unusually simple structure that, while having even fewer parameters than many state-of-the-art CNNs and ViTs, often achieves or even surpasses them for image classification, object detection and various semantic segmentation tasks such as ImageNet, COCO, PASCAL VOC, ADE20K, PanopticFCN, etc. Specifically, the VAN-B6 achieves 87.8% accuracy on ImageNet and even sets a new state-of-the-art for panoptic segmentation with a score of 58.2 PQ. The VAN-B2 surpasses the state-of-the-art Swin-T on the ADE20K semantic segmentation by 4pt (50.1 vs. 46.1 mIoU) and the COCO object detection by 2pt (48.8 vs. 46.2 AP). The experiments here offer a new way of understanding the attention, and meanwhile the VAN can be treated as an easily-accessible and robust baseline for the vision community. Code and models are available at: https://github.com/Visual-Attention-Network.
